frameworks.name: radial bias in early visual cortex
frameworks.one_line: preferential cortical response to orientations aligned with gaze-centered radial axis

frameworks.name: perceptual illusion as readout of early-vision representation
frameworks.one_line: low-level orientation preference biases perceived object orientation

core_question: Does the cortical preference for radial orientations distort the perceived orientation of peripheral objects?
key_assumptions: radially oriented stimuli elicit stronger cortical activity and higher perceptual sensitivity
perceived gap position in a Landolt C reflects the underlying orientation representation
manipulations: stimulus location along four meridians (vertical, horizontal, 45°, 135°)
peripheral placement requiring continuous response of gap position
key_findings: perceived gap was systematically attracted toward the radial axis
a regular C at oblique meridian was often misperceived as tilted toward that meridian
errors followed the radial axis across meridians
interpretations: early-vision radial preference exerts an unsuspected influence on perceived object orientation
